mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.
description: Enrich learning extraction by switching loop iteration spawns to --output-format stream-json, capturing the full conversation (tool calls, results, errors) instead of just the final --print text. All spawns use --no-session-persistence to keep ~/.claude/projects/ clean.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks
criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

userStories.title: Initial tests for stream-json parsing and content formatting
userStories.description: Write tests for the stream-json line parser and content formatter before implementation. Tests use inline JSON fixtures to verify parsing of assistant, user, result, and system message types, content block extraction, truncation, and error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: assistant message with text block extracts text correctly
Test: assistant message with tool_use block formats as [Tool: name] {truncated input}
Test: assistant message with thinking block — thinking is excluded
Test: assistant message with error field set — output includes [Error: {error_text}]
Test: user message with tool_result extracts content (truncated to 1000 chars)
Test: result message extracts result field as the 'output' text
Test: result message with subtype=error — handled without panic, output is result text or empty
Test: result message with null result field — treated as empty string
Test: system message is skipped entirely
Test: tool_use input truncated to 500 chars when longer
Test: tool_result content truncated to 1000 chars when longer
Test: global output capped at 50,000 chars
Test: malformed JSON line is skipped, subsequent valid lines still processed
Known-bad discriminator: test that tool_use input is JSON-serialized (not Display-formatted)
userStories.notes: The parser function should accept an iterator of lines (for testability) rather than reading from a process. Tests can pass Vec<String> as input.
edgeCases: Malformed JSON line — skip and continue
Enormous tool_use input (>500 chars) — truncated with char-boundary safety
Enormous tool_result content (>1000 chars) — truncated with char-boundary safety
Unicode in tool results — truncation must not split multi-byte characters
Empty lines in stream — skip gracefully
assistant message with error field set — include [Error: rate_limit] in output
result message with null result field — treat as empty string
result message with subtype=error — handle gracefully
Stream with only a result message (no assistant messages) — output is just the result text
invariants: Per-block truncation: tool_use ≤ 500 chars, tool_result ≤ 1000 chars
Message ordering preserved from input stream
result.result field always extracted into ClaudeResult.output

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Skip the line, continue processing remaining lines

failureModes.cause: result message missing or result field is null

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Extract result text if present, otherwise empty string

userStories.title: Initial tests for spawn_claude stream_json mode and --no-session-persistence
userStories.description: Write tests for the new spawn_claude stream_json parameter. Tests verify that --output-format stream-json and --no-session-persistence appear in args when stream_json=true, and that --print and --no-session-persistence appear when stream_json=false.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: spawn_claude with stream_json=true includes --output-format stream-json in args
Test: spawn_claude with stream_json=true includes --no-session-persistence in args
Test: spawn_claude with stream_json=true does NOT include --print in args
Test: spawn_claude with stream_json=false includes --print in args (existing behavior)
Test: spawn_claude with stream_json=false includes --no-session-persistence in args
Test: --output-format stream-json and --print are mutually exclusive (never both present)
Known-bad discriminator: test that --output-format stream-json is placed BEFORE -p (not after prompt text)
userStories.notes: Follow existing test patterns in claude.rs (lines 500-1030) which use CLAUDE_BINARY=echo to capture constructed args. Tests may use #[ignore] until implementation lands.
edgeCases: stream_json=true + model=Some — both flags present in correct order
--no-session-persistence absent when it should be present (session files leak)

failureModes.cause: --output-format placed after -p, becoming part of prompt text

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Always present in both stream_json=true and stream_json=false modes

userStories.title: Add stream_json param to spawn_claude signature, update ClaudeResult, update all callers
userStories.description: Add stream_json: bool parameter to spawn_claude. Add conversation: Option<String> to ClaudeResult. When stream_json=true: use --output-format stream-json --no-session-persistence (no --print). When stream_json=false: use --print --no-session-persistence. Update all 4 callers to pass stream_json=false so everything compiles. No behavior change in the tee loop yet — stream-json parsing comes in FEAT-002.
acceptanceCriteria: ClaudeResult has conversation: Option<String> field (set to None for now)
spawn_claude accepts stream_json: bool parameter
When stream_json=true: args include --output-format stream-json --no-session-persistence, no --print
When stream_json=false: args include --print --no-session-persistence
All 4 callers updated to pass stream_json=false
All existing tests pass (update test assertions for new arg: --no-session-persistence)
TEST-INIT-002 tests pass
userStories.notes: This is a compile-fix task — the tee loop still reads lines as plain text. FEAT-002 adds the JSON parsing. conversation is always None at this point.

correctness: --no-session-persistence must appear in args for BOTH stream_json=true and false
performance: No performance impact — just arg construction changes
style: Match existing arg construction pattern in spawn_claude

userStories.title: Implement stream-json line parser as a testable function
userStories.description: Create a pure function that accepts an iterator of stream-json lines and returns (output_text: String, conversation: Option<String>). Parses assistant messages (text blocks → full text, tool_use → [Tool: name] {truncated input 500 chars}, skip thinking), user messages (tool_result → truncated to 1000 chars), result messages (extract result field). Skips system messages and malformed lines. Caps conversation at 50K chars.
acceptanceCriteria: Pure function: accepts iterator of lines, returns (String, Option<String>)
Assistant text blocks included as-is in conversation
Tool_use blocks formatted as [Tool: {name}] {json-serialized-input truncated to 500 chars}
Tool_result content truncated to 1000 chars in conversation
Thinking blocks skipped
System messages skipped
result.result extracted as output text
result with null result field → output is empty string
assistant error field → [Error: {error}] in conversation
Malformed JSON lines skipped with eprintln warning
Conversation capped at 50,000 chars
Per-block truncation does not split mid-character
TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
userStories.notes: Keep this as a standalone function in claude.rs (or a submodule). It should be pure — no I/O, no stderr, just parsing. The caller (tee loop) handles stderr display.

correctness: Malformed JSON lines must be skipped, not crash
Per-block truncation must not split mid-character
result.result must be extracted correctly for detection::analyze_output
performance: JSON parsing per line is fast (<1ms per serde_json::from_str call)
style: Match graceful degradation pattern (skip + warn on malformed lines)

userStories.title: Wire stream-json parser into spawn_claude tee loop
userStories.description: In spawn_claude's tee loop, when stream_json=true: call the parser function from FEAT-002 on collected lines, tee assistant text blocks to stderr for live display, populate ClaudeResult.output from the parser's output_text, and ClaudeResult.conversation from the parser's conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: When stream_json=true: tee loop parses JSON lines and displays assistant text on stderr
When stream_json=true: ClaudeResult.output contains result.result text
When stream_json=true: ClaudeResult.conversation contains formatted conversation
When stream_json=false: existing tee behavior unchanged (lines echoed to stderr, collected into output)
Malformed JSON lines in stream produce eprintln warning but don't crash
userStories.notes: Two approaches for live display: (A) parse each line as it arrives and tee text blocks immediately, or (B) collect all lines and parse at end. Prefer (A) for real-time display. The parser from FEAT-002 can be called per-line or refactored to work incrementally.

correctness: Live display must show text as it arrives, not buffer until end
ClaudeResult.output must match what --print would have produced
performance: No buffering of display — text teed to stderr immediately per line
style: Follow existing tee loop pattern in spawn_claude

userStories.title: Wire engine to use stream_json=true and pass conversation to learning extraction
userStories.description: In engine.rs: change spawn_claude call to pass stream_json=true for loop iterations. Use claude_result.conversation for learning extraction input when available, fall back to claude_result.output. If conversation is Some but empty, still pass it (don't fall back).
acceptanceCriteria: Engine passes stream_json=true to spawn_claude
After iteration, uses claude_result.conversation for learning extraction if Some (even if empty)
Falls back to claude_result.output only if conversation is None
detection::analyze_output still uses claude_result.output (the result text)
Utility spawns (ingestion, curate, enrich) still pass stream_json=false
userStories.notes: detection::analyze_output must continue to use claude_result.output since it looks for specific markers like <completed>. Only the learning extraction input changes. Empty conversation (Some("")) is valid — it means the stream had no useful content beyond the result.

correctness: detection::analyze_output must NOT be changed — it uses result text markers
Fallback to output only when conversation is None, not when empty
performance: No additional I/O — conversation is already in memory from the tee loop
style: Follow existing engine.rs patterns for best-effort operations

userStories.title: Review implementation for quality, security, and integration wiring
userStories.description: Review all FEAT tasks for code quality, error handling, security, and integration wiring. Verify stream-json parsing is robust, arg ordering is correct, conversation field is used, --no-session-persistence is present on ALL spawn calls.
acceptanceCriteria: No unwrap() in production code paths
All errors properly propagated with context
Security: no injection via malformed JSON lines
Quality dimensions met for each task
Edge case coverage: each edgeCases entry has a corresponding test
Integration: engine.rs passes stream_json=true and uses conversation field
Verify: --no-session-persistence present on ALL 4 spawn_claude call sites
Verify: utility spawns (ingestion, curate, enrich) pass stream_json=false
Any issues found have corresponding CODE-FIX-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.notes: For each issue found: add CODE-FIX-xxx task (priority 14-16), add to MILESTONE-1 dependsOn.

userStories.title: Review implementation for refactoring opportunities
userStories.description: Analyze implementation code for DRY violations, complexity, coupling. The stream-json parsing logic and tee loop may be getting complex — check if extraction into a separate module is warranted.
acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication (DRY principle)
Functions under 30 lines (flag complex ones)
No tight coupling between modules
Any issues found have corresponding REFACTOR-1-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.notes: spawn_claude now has two code paths (text vs stream-json) in the tee loop. If this makes the function too long, suggest extracting into a submodule.

CODE-REVIEW-1 passes (and any spawned CODE-FIX-xxx tasks)
REFACTOR-REVIEW-1 passes (and any spawned REFACTOR-1-xxx tasks)

cargo test passes with no failures
cargo fmt --check passes
--no-session-persistence present on all 4 spawn_claude call sites
userStories.notes: Initial tests pass, implementation reviewed, refactored, and fully wired in.

userStories.title: Comprehensive tests for stream-json parsing
userStories.description: Expand test coverage beyond initial tests. Cover parameterized scenarios, boundary conditions, and interaction between truncation levels.
acceptanceCriteria: Parameterized tests (#[rstest]) for multiple message type combinations
Test: interleaved assistant/user/system messages — only relevant ones extracted
Test: truncation at exactly 500/1000 char boundaries (not off-by-one)
Test: Unicode truncation preserves char boundaries
Test: very large conversation (>50K chars) truncated correctly
Test: multiple assistant messages accumulate correctly
Test: assistant message with error field produces [Error: ...] in output
Test: result with subtype=error handled correctly
80%+ coverage for stream-json parsing code

userStories.description: Create an integration test using CLAUDE_BINARY pointing to a mock shell script at tests/fixtures/mock_stream_json.sh that outputs stream-json lines to stdout. Verify spawn_claude parses them correctly and produces the expected ClaudeResult.
acceptanceCriteria: Mock script at tests/fixtures/mock_stream_json.sh outputs valid stream-json lines (assistant + user + result)
